---
title: "`r params$report_title`"
author: "`r params$report_author`"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    theme: flatly
    toc: false
    code_folding: hide
params:
  # Engine flag: TRUE only when this template is stitched into the
  # combined report (set by the parent). Leave false for normal renders.
  combined_mode: false
  # Master Data Path (one metric value per district)
  data_path: !r here::here("1_data", "master_district_indicators.csv")

  # Geometry + versioned name crosswalk. Bare filenames resolved under
  # 1_data/geo/ via here() below (same pattern as `data` in render_config.yml),
  # so render_config.yml can override them with plain filenames.
  geo_file: "pk_districts_demo.geojson"
  lookup_file: "district_lookup.csv"
  geo_key: "district"        # the name field inside the geojson to join on

  # Variables (validated against the DATA columns)
  region_var: "district"     # district-name column in the data
  value_var: "literacy_rate" # metric to shade

  # Classing: number of quantile bins for the fill (design ramp is fixed)
  n_bins: 5

  # Policy-Facing Labels
  metric_name: "Adult Literacy Rate (%)"

  # --- Report Text (all editable; leave "" to use the smart default) ---
  report_title: "District Map: Choropleth"
  report_author: "Ibraheem Saqib Ellahi"
  chart_title: ""
  chart_subtitle: ""
  source_note: "Generated via CERP Analytics Template"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = !isTRUE(params$combined_mode),
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  dev.args = list(bg = "#FAF9F6"),
  fig.path = paste0(here::here("4_output", "figures"), "/"),
  fig.width = 8,
  fig.height = 7,
  fig.retina = 3
)
```

```{r cerp-style, echo=FALSE, results='asis', eval=!isTRUE(params$combined_mode)}
cat("<style>", readr::read_file(here::here("2_R", "cerp_style.css")), "</style>")
```

```{r load-and-prep}
library(tidyverse)
source(here::here("2_R", "2.5_helpers.R"), local = TRUE)

if (!requireNamespace("sf", quietly = TRUE)) {
  stop("The 'sf' package is required for the choropleth map. ",
       "Install it once with renv::install('sf') (needs system GDAL/GEOS/PROJ).",
       call. = FALSE)
}
suppressMessages(library(sf))   # attach so dplyr's sf-aware join dispatches

# 1. Load the metric data (headers/values whitespace-scrubbed) and validate that
#    every *_var param names a real DATA column.
raw_data <- cerp_load(params$data_path)
cerp_validate(raw_data, params[grepl("_var$", names(params))])

# Resolve the spatial assets (bare filenames -> absolute paths under 1_data/geo/)
geo_path    <- here::here("1_data", "geo", params$geo_file)
lookup_path <- here::here("1_data", "geo", params$lookup_file)

# 2. Load the geometry and confirm the join key exists inside it.
if (!file.exists(geo_path)) {
  stop("Geometry file not found: ", geo_path,
       "\nCheck the 'geo_file' parameter.", call. = FALSE)
}
geo <- sf::st_read(geo_path, quiet = TRUE)
if (!(params$geo_key %in% names(geo))) {
  stop("geo_key '", params$geo_key, "' is not a field in the geometry. ",
       "Fields available: ", paste(setdiff(names(geo), "geometry"), collapse = " | "),
       call. = FALSE)
}

# 3. Load the versioned crosswalk (skips its leading '#' comment header).
lookup <- NULL
if (file.exists(lookup_path)) {
  lookup <- readr::read_csv(lookup_path, comment = "#",
                            show_col_types = FALSE, trim_ws = TRUE)
  names(lookup) <- stringr::str_squish(names(lookup))
}

# ==============================================================================
# DISTRICT-NAME HARMONIZATION
# Resolve messy data names -> canonical geometry names. Fails LOUDLY (with
# closest-match suggestions) on any district it cannot place, so a map is never
# silently drawn with a district missing.
# ==============================================================================
canonical <- as.character(geo[[params$geo_key]])
prepped <- raw_data %>%
  drop_na(all_of(c(params$region_var, params$value_var))) %>%
  mutate(
    Canon = cerp_harmonize(.data[[params$region_var]], canonical, lookup),
    Value = cerp_numeric(.data[[params$value_var]], params$value_var)
  ) %>%
  # One value per district (mean guards against accidental duplicate rows).
  group_by(Canon) %>%
  summarize(Value = mean(Value, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

# 4. Attach the metric onto the geometry BY MATCH, not left_join(): a dplyr join
#    can silently strip the sf class (dropping the drawable geometry) so the
#    legend renders but the polygons come out blank. match() writes a plain
#    column and never demotes the sf object. Unmatched districts stay NA -> grey.
map_data <- geo
map_data$Value <- prepped$Value[
  match(as.character(map_data[[params$geo_key]]), prepped$Canon)]
map_data <- sf::st_as_sf(map_data)   # belt-and-suspenders: guarantee sf class

# 5. Quantile classing into readable bins (design ramp applied in the plot).
vals <- map_data$Value[!is.na(map_data$Value)]
n_bins <- max(2, min(params$n_bins, length(unique(vals))))
breaks <- unique(quantile(vals, probs = seq(0, 1, length.out = n_bins + 1),
                          na.rm = TRUE, type = 7))
if (length(breaks) < 3) {                     # near-constant data: single class
  map_data$Bin <- factor(ifelse(is.na(map_data$Value), NA,
                                sprintf("%.0f", map_data$Value)))
} else {
  labs_bins <- paste0(round(head(breaks, -1)), "–", round(breaks[-1]))
  map_data$Bin <- cut(map_data$Value, breaks = breaks, labels = labs_bins,
                      include.lowest = TRUE)
}
n_missing <- sum(is.na(map_data$Value))
```


```{r plot-choropleth}
source(here::here("2_R", "2.0_setup_theme.R"), local = TRUE)

# Sequential ramp: pale box tint (low) -> steel blue (high), Design.md palette.
n_lev <- nlevels(map_data$Bin)
ramp <- colorRampPalette(c(cerp_cols[["box"]], cerp_cols[["primary"]]))(max(1, n_lev))

sub_default <- paste0(
  "Darker shading means higher ", tolower(params$metric_name), ".",
  if (n_missing > 0) paste0(" Grey districts have no data (", n_missing, ").") else ""
)

ggplot(map_data) +

  geom_sf(aes(fill = Bin), color = cerp_cols[["bg"]], linewidth = 0.25) +

  scale_fill_manual(
    values = ramp,
    na.value = "#e6e2d9",         # warm grey = "no data"
    name = params$metric_name,
    drop = FALSE,
    guide = guide_legend(direction = "horizontal", nrow = 1,
                         label.position = "bottom", keywidth = unit(1.4, "cm"))
  ) +

  coord_sf(expand = FALSE) +

  theme_cerp() +

  theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    legend.justification = "left",
    legend.title = element_text(family = cerp_font_caption, size = 10,
                                color = cerp_cols[["subtle"]]),
    axis.text = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    panel.grid = element_blank()
  ) +

  labs(
    title = if (nzchar(params$chart_title)) params$chart_title else paste("District Map:", params$metric_name),
    subtitle = if (nzchar(params$chart_subtitle)) params$chart_subtitle else sub_default,
    caption = params$source_note
  )
```
